instruction: Show TypeScript configuration patterns using dotenv, typed config objects, Zod schema validation, and hierarchical config.
output:
<think>
TypeScript config management needs: type safety, runtime validation (env vars are strings), hierarchical overrides, and fail-fast at startup.

The modern stack:
1. dotenv: loads .env files
2. Zod: runtime schema validation with type inference
3. Type inference from schema (no duplication)
4. Hierarchical: base + environment-specific overrides

Zod is superior to manual parsing because it validates AND infers TypeScript types from the same schema — single source of truth.
</think>

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';
import dotenv from 'dotenv';
import path from 'path';

// Load .env file
dotenv.config();

// === Schema-driven config with Zod ===

const serverSchema = z.object({
  HOST: z.string().default('0.0.0.0'),
  PORT: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(65535).default(3000),
  NODE_ENV: z.enum(['development', 'staging', 'production']).default('development'),
  LOG_LEVEL: z.enum(['debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error']).default('info'),
});

const databaseSchema = z.object({
  DATABASE_URL: z.string().url(),
  DB_POOL_MIN: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).default(5),
  DB_POOL_MAX: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).default(20),
  DB_SSL: z.coerce.boolean().default(false),
});

const redisSchema = z.object({
  REDIS_URL: z.string().url().default('redis://localhost:6379'),
  REDIS_TTL: z.coerce.number().int().min(0).default(3600),
});

const authSchema = z.object({
  JWT_SECRET: z.string().min(32, 'JWT_SECRET must be at least 32 chars'),
  JWT_EXPIRY: z.string().default('1h'),
  ALLOWED_ORIGINS: z.string()
    .transform(s => s.split(',').map(o => o.trim()))
    .default('http://localhost:3000'),
});

// Combined schema
const configSchema = z.object({
  ...serverSchema.shape,
  ...databaseSchema.shape,
  ...redisSchema.shape,
  ...authSchema.shape,
}).refine(
  (data) => data.DB_POOL_MAX >= data.DB_POOL_MIN,
  { message: 'DB_POOL_MAX must be >= DB_POOL_MIN', path: ['DB_POOL_MAX'] },
).refine(
  (data) => {
    if (data.NODE_ENV === 'production' && data.JWT_SECRET === 'change-me') {
      return false;
    }
    return true;
  },
  { message: 'Must set JWT_SECRET in production', path: ['JWT_SECRET'] },
);

// Infer type from schema — single source of truth
type RawConfig = z.infer<typeof configSchema>;

// Structured config object (nice API for consumers)
export interface AppConfig {
  server: {
    host: string;
    port: number;
    env: 'development' | 'staging' | 'production';
    logLevel: 'debug' | 'info' | 'warn' | 'error';
  };
  database: {
    url: string;
    poolMin: number;
    poolMax: number;
    ssl: boolean;
  };
  redis: {
    url: string;
    ttl: number;
  };
  auth: {
    jwtSecret: string;
    jwtExpiry: string;
    allowedOrigins: string[];
  };
}

function transformConfig(raw: RawConfig): AppConfig {
  return {
    server: {
      host: raw.HOST,
      port: raw.PORT,
      env: raw.NODE_ENV,
      logLevel: raw.LOG_LEVEL,
    },
    database: {
      url: raw.DATABASE_URL,
      poolMin: raw.DB_POOL_MIN,
      poolMax: raw.DB_POOL_MAX,
      ssl: raw.DB_SSL,
    },
    redis: {
      url: raw.REDIS_URL,
      ttl: raw.REDIS_TTL,
    },
    auth: {
      jwtSecret: raw.JWT_SECRET,
      jwtExpiry: raw.JWT_EXPIRY,
      allowedOrigins: raw.ALLOWED_ORIGINS as unknown as string[],
    },
  };
}

// Load + validate + transform
export function loadConfig(): AppConfig {
  const result = configSchema.safeParse(process.env);

  if (!result.success) {
    const errors = result.error.issues
      .map(i => `  ${i.path.join('.')}: ${i.message}`)
      .join('\n');
    console.error(`Config validation failed:\n${errors}`);
    process.exit(1);
  }

  return transformConfig(result.data);
}

// Singleton
let _config: AppConfig | null = null;

export function getConfig(): AppConfig {
  if (!_config) {
    _config = loadConfig();
  }
  return _config;
}
```

instruction: Show how to implement hierarchical TypeScript configuration with base + environment overrides and type-safe deep merging.
output:
<think>
Hierarchical config means: base config provides defaults, environment-specific configs override specific values. Like: config/default.ts + config/production.ts.

Challenges in TypeScript:
1. Deep merging objects while maintaining types
2. Ensuring environment configs only override existing keys (no typos)
3. Type safety — the merged result should have the full type
4. Supporting nested overrides without replacing entire sections

I'll use DeepPartial for overrides and a type-safe deep merge function.
</think>

```typescript
// === Type-safe deep merge utility ===

type DeepPartial<T> = {
  [P in keyof T]?: T[P] extends object ? DeepPartial<T[P]> : T[P];
};

function deepMerge<T extends Record<string, any>>(
  base: T,
  override: DeepPartial<T>,
): T {
  const result = { ...base };
  for (const key of Object.keys(override) as (keyof T)[]) {
    const overrideVal = override[key];
    if (overrideVal === undefined) continue;
    if (
      typeof overrideVal === 'object' &&
      overrideVal !== null &&
      !Array.isArray(overrideVal) &&
      typeof base[key] === 'object'
    ) {
      result[key] = deepMerge(
        base[key] as any,
        overrideVal as any,
      );
    } else {
      result[key] = overrideVal as T[keyof T];
    }
  }
  return result;
}

// === Config type definition ===

interface Config {
  app: {
    name: string;
    version: string;
  };
  server: {
    host: string;
    port: number;
    cors: {
      origins: string[];
      credentials: boolean;
    };
  };
  database: {
    host: string;
    port: number;
    name: string;
    pool: { min: number; max: number };
    ssl: boolean;
  };
  logging: {
    level: string;
    format: 'json' | 'text';
    file?: string;
  };
  features: {
    enableCache: boolean;
    enableMetrics: boolean;
    enableRateLimit: boolean;
    rateLimitRPS: number;
  };
}

// === Base config (defaults) ===

const baseConfig: Config = {
  app: { name: 'MyService', version: '1.0.0' },
  server: {
    host: '0.0.0.0',
    port: 3000,
    cors: { origins: ['http://localhost:3000'], credentials: false },
  },
  database: {
    host: 'localhost',
    port: 5432,
    name: 'myapp_dev',
    pool: { min: 2, max: 10 },
    ssl: false,
  },
  logging: { level: 'debug', format: 'text' },
  features: {
    enableCache: false,
    enableMetrics: false,
    enableRateLimit: false,
    rateLimitRPS: 100,
  },
};

// === Environment overrides (typed as DeepPartial) ===

const environments: Record<string, DeepPartial<Config>> = {
  development: {
    // No overrides — use all defaults
  },
  staging: {
    server: { port: 8080 },
    database: {
      host: 'staging-db.internal',
      name: 'myapp_staging',
      pool: { max: 20 },
      ssl: true,
    },
    logging: { level: 'info', format: 'json' },
    features: { enableCache: true, enableMetrics: true },
  },
  production: {
    server: {
      port: 8080,
      cors: { origins: ['https://myapp.com'], credentials: true },
    },
    database: {
      host: 'prod-db.internal',
      name: 'myapp_prod',
      pool: { min: 10, max: 50 },
      ssl: true,
    },
    logging: { level: 'warn', format: 'json', file: '/var/log/myapp.log' },
    features: {
      enableCache: true,
      enableMetrics: true,
      enableRateLimit: true,
      rateLimitRPS: 1000,
    },
  },
};

// === Load config for current environment ===

export function loadConfig(env?: string): Config {
  const environment = env ?? process.env.NODE_ENV ?? 'development';
  const envOverrides = environments[environment] ?? {};

  const config = deepMerge(baseConfig, envOverrides);

  // Apply any remaining env var overrides
  if (process.env.PORT) {
    config.server.port = parseInt(process.env.PORT, 10);
  }
  if (process.env.DATABASE_URL) {
    // Parse DATABASE_URL into components if needed
  }

  console.log(`Loaded config for ${environment}`);
  return config;
}

// Usage:
// const config = loadConfig();  // Uses NODE_ENV
// console.log(config.database.host);  // Type-safe access
```

instruction: Show TypeScript patterns for runtime configuration validation using Zod with custom error messages and transform pipelines.
output:
<think>
Zod is the go-to runtime validation library for TypeScript. For config validation, key Zod features:

1. Coercion: env vars are strings, Zod can coerce to numbers/booleans
2. Transforms: parse and transform in one step
3. Custom error messages: make validation failures actionable
4. Refinements: cross-field validation
5. Defaults: provide sensible defaults
6. Preprocess: clean up data before validation

I'll show advanced Zod patterns for config: custom types, transforms, error formatting, and composable schemas.
</think>

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';

// === Custom Zod types for config ===

// Duration string: "30s", "5m", "1h" -> milliseconds
const duration = z.string().transform((val, ctx) => {
  const match = val.match(/^(\d+)(ms|s|m|h)$/);
  if (!match) {
    ctx.addIssue({
      code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
      message: `Invalid duration "${val}". Use: 30s, 5m, 1h`,
    });
    return z.NEVER;
  }
  const [, num, unit] = match;
  const multipliers: Record<string, number> = { ms: 1, s: 1000, m: 60000, h: 3600000 };
  return parseInt(num) * multipliers[unit];
});

// Byte size: "10MB", "1GB" -> bytes
const byteSize = z.string().transform((val, ctx) => {
  const match = val.match(/^(\d+)(KB|MB|GB)$/i);
  if (!match) {
    ctx.addIssue({
      code: z.ZodIssueCode.custom,
      message: `Invalid size "${val}". Use: 10KB, 50MB, 1GB`,
    });
    return z.NEVER;
  }
  const [, num, unit] = match;
  const multipliers: Record<string, number> = {
    KB: 1024, MB: 1024 ** 2, GB: 1024 ** 3,
  };
  return parseInt(num) * multipliers[unit.toUpperCase()];
});

// Comma-separated list -> array
const commaSeparated = z.string().transform(s =>
  s.split(',').map(item => item.trim()).filter(Boolean)
);

// Port number from string
const port = z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(65535);

// Boolean from various string representations
const booleanish = z.preprocess((val) => {
  if (typeof val === 'string') {
    const lower = val.toLowerCase();
    if (['true', '1', 'yes', 'on'].includes(lower)) return true;
    if (['false', '0', 'no', 'off'].includes(lower)) return false;
  }
  return val;
}, z.boolean());

// === Composable config schema ===

const configSchema = z.object({
  NODE_ENV: z.enum(['development', 'test', 'staging', 'production'])
    .default('development'),

  PORT: port.default(3000),

  DATABASE_URL: z.string().url({
    message: 'DATABASE_URL must be a valid URL (e.g., postgres://user:pass@host/db)',
  }),

  DB_POOL_SIZE: z.coerce.number().int().min(1).max(100).default(10),

  REDIS_URL: z.string().url().default('redis://localhost:6379'),

  JWT_SECRET: z.string().min(32, {
    message: 'JWT_SECRET must be at least 32 characters for security',
  }),

  JWT_EXPIRY: duration.default('1h'),

  REQUEST_TIMEOUT: duration.default('30s'),

  MAX_UPLOAD_SIZE: byteSize.default('10MB'),

  ALLOWED_ORIGINS: commaSeparated.default('http://localhost:3000'),

  ENABLE_CACHE: booleanish.default(false),
  ENABLE_METRICS: booleanish.default(false),

  LOG_LEVEL: z.enum(['debug', 'info', 'warn', 'error']).default('info'),

}).refine(
  (data) => {
    if (data.NODE_ENV === 'production') {
      return data.JWT_SECRET !== 'development-secret';
    }
    return true;
  },
  {
    message: 'Change JWT_SECRET from default in production!',
    path: ['JWT_SECRET'],
  },
);

type Config = z.infer<typeof configSchema>;

// === Pretty error formatting ===

function formatZodErrors(error: z.ZodError): string {
  return error.issues
    .map((issue) => {
      const path = issue.path.join('.') || 'root';
      return `  ${path}: ${issue.message}`;
    })
    .join('\n');
}

// === Load and validate ===

export function loadConfig(): Config {
  const result = configSchema.safeParse(process.env);

  if (!result.success) {
    console.error('\nConfiguration Error:');
    console.error(formatZodErrors(result.error));
    console.error('\nCheck your .env file or environment variables.\n');
    process.exit(1);
  }

  const config = result.data;

  // Log loaded config (mask secrets)
  console.log('Config loaded:', {
    env: config.NODE_ENV,
    port: config.PORT,
    db: config.DATABASE_URL.replace(/\/\/.*@/, '//<redacted>@'),
    cache: config.ENABLE_CACHE,
    metrics: config.ENABLE_METRICS,
  });

  return config;
}
```

instruction: Show how to implement a typed configuration system in TypeScript that supports multiple file formats (JSON, YAML, TOML) with schema validation.
output:
<think>
A multi-format config loader needs:
1. Detect format from file extension
2. Parse each format
3. Validate against a schema
4. Merge multiple config sources
5. Environment variable overrides

I'll build a ConfigLoader class that handles JSON, YAML, and TOML, validates with Zod, and supports layered loading.
</think>

```typescript
import { z } from 'zod';
import * as fs from 'fs';
import * as path from 'path';
import * as yaml from 'yaml';
import * as toml from '@iarna/toml';

type FileFormat = 'json' | 'yaml' | 'toml';

function detectFormat(filePath: string): FileFormat {
  const ext = path.extname(filePath).toLowerCase();
  switch (ext) {
    case '.json': return 'json';
    case '.yaml': case '.yml': return 'yaml';
    case '.toml': return 'toml';
    default: throw new Error(`Unsupported config format: ${ext}`);
  }
}

function parseFile(filePath: string): Record<string, unknown> {
  const content = fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8');
  const format = detectFormat(filePath);

  switch (format) {
    case 'json': return JSON.parse(content);
    case 'yaml': return yaml.parse(content);
    case 'toml': return toml.parse(content) as Record<string, unknown>;
  }
}

function deepMerge(base: Record<string, any>, override: Record<string, any>): Record<string, any> {
  const result = { ...base };
  for (const [key, val] of Object.entries(override)) {
    if (val && typeof val === 'object' && !Array.isArray(val) && typeof result[key] === 'object') {
      result[key] = deepMerge(result[key], val);
    } else {
      result[key] = val;
    }
  }
  return result;
}

// Generic config loader
class ConfigLoader<T> {
  private schema: z.ZodType<T>;
  private layers: Record<string, unknown>[] = [];

  constructor(schema: z.ZodType<T>) {
    this.schema = schema;
  }

  addFile(filePath: string, required = false): this {
    if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
      this.layers.push(parseFile(filePath));
    } else if (required) {
      throw new Error(`Required config file not found: ${filePath}`);
    }
    return this;
  }

  addObject(obj: Record<string, unknown>): this {
    this.layers.push(obj);
    return this;
  }

  addEnv(prefix: string, mapping: Record<string, string>): this {
    const envValues: Record<string, unknown> = {};
    for (const [envKey, configPath] of Object.entries(mapping)) {
      const fullKey = `${prefix}_${envKey}`;
      const value = process.env[fullKey];
      if (value !== undefined) {
        const parts = configPath.split('.');
        let current: any = envValues;
        for (let i = 0; i < parts.length - 1; i++) {
          current[parts[i]] = current[parts[i]] || {};
          current = current[parts[i]];
        }
        current[parts[parts.length - 1]] = value;
      }
    }
    if (Object.keys(envValues).length > 0) {
      this.layers.push(envValues);
    }
    return this;
  }

  load(): T {
    let merged: Record<string, unknown> = {};
    for (const layer of this.layers) {
      merged = deepMerge(merged, layer as Record<string, any>);
    }
    const result = this.schema.safeParse(merged);
    if (!result.success) {
      const errors = result.error.issues
        .map(i => `  ${i.path.join('.')}: ${i.message}`)
        .join('\n');
      throw new Error(`Config validation failed:\n${errors}`);
    }
    return result.data;
  }
}

// Usage
const appSchema = z.object({
  app_name: z.string().default('MyService'),
  environment: z.enum(['dev', 'staging', 'prod']).default('dev'),
  server: z.object({
    host: z.string().default('0.0.0.0'),
    port: z.coerce.number().default(3000),
  }),
  database: z.object({
    host: z.string(),
    port: z.coerce.number().default(5432),
    name: z.string(),
  }),
});

const config = new ConfigLoader(appSchema)
  .addFile('config/default.toml')     // Base config
  .addFile('config/production.yaml')  // Env-specific override
  .addEnv('APP', {
    'SERVER_PORT': 'server.port',
    'DB_HOST': 'database.host',
    'DB_NAME': 'database.name',
  })
  .load();

console.log(config.server.port);  // Fully typed
```
